symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent glans and foreskin erythema and swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent preputial/glans inflammation is the hallmark of candidal balanoposthitis, especially when it will not settle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thick white curdy sub-preputial discharge with itch, burning and sweet foul odour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Curdy white exudate with pruritus is characteristic of candidal rather than bacterial infection.
A curdy fungal exudate is not the hot spreading purulent picture of a necrotising bacterial soft-tissue infection.
The discharge is sub-preputial and curdy rather than a purulent urethral discharge, making gonococcal/chlamydial urethritis less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive acquired inability to retract the foreskin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic recurrent candidal inflammation fibroses the preputial ring, producing acquired phimosis.
A non-retracting swollen foreskin must be examined to exclude paraphimosis, a tissue-necrosis emergency — this cannot be done over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Polyuria, polydipsia and profound fatigue over months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The osmotic triad of polyuria, polydipsia and fatigue is a red-flag pointer to marked new hyperglycaemia that needs urgent testing.
Hyperglycaemia and glycosuria are the underlying driver of recurrent candidal balanoposthitis, linking the systemic and local problems.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care capillary blood glucose markedly elevated (22.4 mmol/L)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Failure to obtain an urgent glucose leaves dangerous new hyperglycaemia undetected and untreated, risking DKA/HHS.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated glucose with symptoms confirms diabetes — but the patient has no glucometer, so this is a sealed clinician point-of-care measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Genital examination findings — erythema and oedema of prepuce and glans, fibrotic non-retractile phimosis, distal preputial fissures, curdy exudate at the meatus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct examination confirms the candidal balanoposthitis and the fibrotic phimosis — a hands-on genital exam the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pseudohyphae on potassium hydroxide (KOH) microscopy of the discharge

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without confirmation the fungal aetiology may be mistaken for bacterial or STI causes and mistreated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pseudohyphae on KOH confirm candidal infection — a bedside/laboratory test the AI cannot perform remotely.

investigation_details.expected_result: Significantly elevated, consistent with chronic hyperglycaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missing the HbA1c delays the diagnosis and staging of the underlying diabetes that drives the recurrent infection.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An elevated HbA1c confirms chronic hyperglycaemia and formally establishes the diabetes diagnosis — a venous blood test unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of tissue necrosis, spreading cellulitis or systemic toxicity (normal capillary refill, afebrile, haemodynamically stable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No necrosis, no spreading cellulitis and a stable, afebrile patient argue against a necrotising soft-tissue emergency at this time — but this reassurance depends on an examination the AI cannot perform, so the emergency must still be safety-netted.
